_comment_purpose: Recommended mode: single server cron, no agent cron needed. The worker uses `task_create_task_blocking` to dispatch the ip task directly to each mapping.agent_uuid and wait for the result, then PATCHes Cloudflare.
_comment_cron_format: 6-segment: sec min hr day mon weekday.

server_worker_cron._comment_params: js_worker[1] is onCron's `params`. {} = sync all mappings, {only:'name'} = single mapping, {force:true} = bypass KV unchanged-short-circuit.

_optional_daily_force._comment: Once a day at 03:00, force re-push for all mappings (defends against silent CF dashboard edits faster than HEARTBEAT_HOURS).

_optional_only_one_mapping._comment: If you want different cadences per mapping (e.g. NAS every 15min, home every 5min), register an extra per-mapping server cron with `only`.

_optional_mappings_in_params._comment: Carry the entire MAPPINGS array INSIDE the cron's params instead of env.MAPPINGS. The worker uses params.mappings as a full replacement of env.MAPPINGS for this run. CF_API_TOKEN and SHARED_SECRET STAY IN ENV — never embed those here (they would be persisted in crontab_result history). Useful for: multi-tenant single worker, A/B test a new mapping without redeploying, ad-hoc isolated cron group.
